# Project Analysis — Flux Image-to-Image Feasibility

**Generated:** 2026-04-23
**Generated By:** @architect (Aria)
**Task:** `analyze-project-structure.md` (WIS-15, Phase 1 of Incremental Feature Workflow)
**Feature under investigation:** Flux **image-to-image** — user submits (image + prompt) → new image generated by a Flux model family member.

> **Scope note.** This document is a read-only inventory of the current servegate stack so we can plan the i2i extension without breaking T2I. Recommendations live in the companion [`recommended-approach.md`](./recommended-approach.md).

---

## 1. Project Structure

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Repository | `servegate` (public, Jhonata-Matias/servegate) + `servegate-planning` (private mirror) |
| Framework | AIOX-FullStack (story-driven, multi-agent) |
| Primary language | TypeScript (gateway + SDK + web) + Python (serverless handler) |
| Runtime layers | Cloudflare Workers (edge gateway) · RunPod Serverless (GPU inference) · Vercel (landing) |
| Testing | Vitest (TS), pytest (Python), smoke/bench scripts |
| API contract (since 2026-04-23) | Async submit/poll — `POST /jobs` (202) + `GET /jobs/{id}` (202/200) per ADR-0002 |
| SDK version | `@jhonata-matias/flux-client@0.2.0` (public on GitHub Packages) |
| Current model | **FLUX.1-schnell** (distilled, 4-step, fp8_e4m3fn) |

---

## 2. Service Inventory

| Service | Path | Language | Entry point | Tests | README |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Gateway** | `gateway/` | TypeScript (Workers) | `src/index.ts` | `tests/` (vitest) | — (see `wrangler.toml`) |
| **SDK** | `sdk/` | TypeScript (ESM + CJS) | `src/index.ts` → `dist/` | `tests/` (vitest) | ✅ |
| **Serverless handler** | `serverless/` | Python 3 | `handler.py` | `tests/` (smoke/bench) | ✅ |
| **Docs portal** | `web/docs/` | Astro + Starlight (MDX, TS strict) | `src/content/docs/index.mdx` | Lighthouse + axe + linkinator | ✅ |
| **Legacy landing** _(archived 2026-04-29 per Story 2.9)_ | `web/_archive/landing-pre-docs-2026-04-29/` | Static HTML/JS | `index.html` | replaced by docs portal | ✅ |
| **Examples** | `examples/colab/` | Python (notebook) | — | — | ✅ |

Two additional AIOX-internal directories (`squads/`, `.aiox-core/`) are framework tooling, out of scope for this analysis.

---

## 3. Data Flow (current — text-to-image)

```
Client (SDK v0.2.0)
  └── POST /jobs  { prompt, steps, seed, width, height }
         │
         ▼
Cloudflare Worker Gateway  (gateway/src/index.ts)
  ├── validateAuth (X-API-Key)                           ← auth.ts
  ├── checkAndIncrement rate-limit (100/day global)      ← rate-limit.ts (KV)
  ├── submitJob → RunPod /run                            ← runpod.ts
  ├── crypto.randomUUID() → job_id
  ├── putMapping(JOBS_KV, {job_id ⇄ runpod_request_id})  ← storage.ts
  └── 202 Accepted { job_id, status_url, est_wait_seconds:"unknown" }
         │
         ▼ (SDK polls every 5s, Retry-After header)
GET /jobs/{id}
  ├── validateAuth
  ├── checkAndRead rate-limit (EC-5: GET does NOT consume quota)
  ├── getMapping(JOBS_KV, job_id)
  ├── getStatus → RunPod /status/{runpod_request_id}
  └── mapStatus → queued|running (202) | completed (200 + output) | failed|cancelled (500) | timeout (504)
         │
         ▼
RunPod Serverless Worker  (serverless/handler.py)
  ├── wait_for_comfy (COMFY_HOST=127.0.0.1:8188, 120s boot timeout)
  ├── normalize_input { prompt, steps, seed, width, height } ← validation: 256-2048 ×8, 1-50 steps
  ├── build_workflow → ComfyUI graph (8 nodes, FLUX.1-schnell)
  ├── queue_workflow (POST /prompt)
  ├── poll_history (280s timeout, 0.25s interval)
  ├── fetch_image (GET /view)
  └── return { image_b64, metadata: { seed, elapsed_ms } }
```

**Architectural invariants (binding contracts):**

- **CON-3** (ADR-0002): `serverless/handler.py` is a black-box to the gateway — gateway treats RunPod input as opaque JSON passthrough. Any schema change happens at the handler; gateway forwards.
- **CON-4**: gateway NEVER logs prompt content or image bytes. Only metadata (timestamps, status codes, job_ids, ip, elapsed).
- **AD-1** (ADR-0002): `/jobs/{id}` response carries `image_b64` inline in `output` — no post-completion fetch round-trip required.
- **NFR-4**: `job_id` is UUID v4 (non-enumerable).

---

## 4. Current ComfyUI Workflow (text-to-image, 8 nodes)

Defined in `serverless/handler.py:build_workflow()` (also mirrored in `serverless/workflow_template.json`):

| Node ID | Class | Purpose | Input binding |
|---|---|---|---|
| 10 | `UNETLoader` | Load `flux1-schnell.safetensors` | weight_dtype: `fp8_e4m3fn` |
| 11 | `DualCLIPLoader` | Load `t5xxl_fp8_e4m3fn` + `clip_l` | type: `flux` |
| 12 | `VAELoader` | Load `ae.safetensors` | — |
| 13 | **`EmptyLatentImage`** ⚠️ | Create empty latent (width × height) | `batch_size: 1` |
| 14 | `CLIPTextEncode` | Positive prompt → conditioning | from `${prompt}` |
| 15 | `CLIPTextEncode` | Negative prompt (empty string) | — |
| 16 | `KSampler` | Sample from latent | `cfg: 1.0`, `euler`, `simple`, `denoise: 1.0` |
| 17 | `VAEDecode` | Latent → pixel | — |
| 18 | `SaveImage` | Write PNG to output dir | `filename_prefix: out` |

**⚠️ Node 13 is the key swap point for i2i.** Text-to-image starts from an empty latent (`EmptyLatentImage`); image-to-image replaces this with a path that encodes the input image into latent space (`LoadImage` → `VAEEncode`). The `KSampler` `denoise` parameter (currently `1.0` — full denoise = pure T2I) drops below `1.0` to control how much of the input image is preserved.

---

## 5. Public Input Contract

### Current (T2I)

```ts
// sdk/src/types.ts:GenerateInput
interface GenerateInput {
  prompt: string;        // required, non-empty
  steps: number;         // 1-50, default 4
  width: number;         // 256-2048, multiple of 8, default 1024
  height: number;        // 256-2048, multiple of 8, default 1024
  seed?: number;         // 0 ≤ seed < 2^63, default random uint31
}

interface GenerateMetadata { seed: number; elapsed_ms: number; }
interface GenerateOutput { output: { image_b64: string; metadata: GenerateMetadata } }
```

### Gateway passthrough

- `gateway/src/index.ts:handleSubmit` parses body as `Record<string, unknown>` and forwards verbatim to RunPod — zero validation at the edge. **Validation lives exclusively in `serverless/handler.py:normalize_input`**.
- Implication for i2i: adding `init_image` to the schema requires **zero gateway changes** (modulo payload-size limits — see §7).

---

## 6. Language & Pattern Distribution

| Dimension | Detail |
|---|---|
| TypeScript | `gateway/`, `sdk/`, `web/docs/` — strict `tsc --noEmit` (web/docs/ via `astro check`) |
| Python | `serverless/` — no type checker wired; tests via pytest |
| Testing frameworks | vitest (TS), pytest (Py) |
| Documentation style | README per module + `docs/usage/*.md`, `docs/api/*.md`, `docs/architecture/adr-*.md`, `docs/stories/*.story.md` |
| Config | `.env` (gateway secrets via `wrangler secret put`), env vars in RunPod template, `core-config.yaml` (AIOX) |
| CI | `.github/workflows/` — minimal (TD8 documented in Epic 2 v0.7) |
| Error handling | Typed errors in SDK (`TimeoutError`, `RateLimitError`, `AuthError`, `ValidationError`, `NetworkError`); status-code mapping in gateway (`handleUpstreamError`); Python handler returns `{error, code}` shape |

---

## 7. Cross-Cutting Constraints Relevant to i2i

These surfaces will be touched by any i2i implementation and must be respected:

| Surface | Constraint | Source | i2i impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Cloudflare Workers request body** | Hard limit 100 MB; soft recommendation ≤10 MB | Cloudflare platform | Base64-encoded input image up to ~7 MB fits comfortably; consider URL-reference as alternative |
| **RunPod `/run` payload** | Serverless payload limits apply (RunPod doc: 20 MB) | RunPod Serverless | Same tradeoff — base64 vs pre-uploaded URL |
| **GPU VRAM** | RTX 4090 = 24 GB (current deploy target, US-IL-1 datacenter-locked by network volume) | `serverless/README.md`; ADR-0001 | FLUX.1-Kontext-dev is 12B bfloat16 (~24 GB raw) — requires fp8/int4 quantization to fit, same pattern as current schnell fp8 |
| **Cold start** | First-invocation after idle ~130s (Path A accepted in ADR-0001) | ADR-0001 | Adding a larger UNet or extra nodes widens cold start; risk of crossing 180s timeout. 30-day review triggers (Epic 2 §30-Day Review Governance) still apply |
| **Rate-limit quota** | 100 images/day global across all users | Story 2.5 | i2i jobs consume the same pool — budget needs revisit if i2i doubles typical session count |
| **Async contract** | `POST /jobs` returns 202; client polls `GET /jobs/{id}` | ADR-0002 | i2i reuses the same contract — no migration required |
| **Logging PII** | CON-4: never log prompt or image bytes | ADR-0002 | i2i input image is also PII-class — handler MUST NOT log bytes or base64 blobs |
| **License** | servegate is MIT; model weights carry their own license | `README.md`, `sdk/LICENSE` | **FLUX.1-dev family (incl. Kontext-dev) is Non-Commercial** — blocker for eventual commercialization path. See recommended-approach.md §License Gate |

---

## 8. Existing Stories & ADRs — Precedent Map

| Artifact | What it establishes | Relevance to i2i |
|---|---|---|
| Story 1.1 — `install-comfyui-flux.story.md` | Pod self-hosted ComfyUI + FLUX.1-schnell baseline | Reference for ComfyUI graph patterns |
| Story 2.1 — `runpod-serverless-flux-endpoint` | Current RunPod handler + Dockerfile + deploy | Target for handler.py extension |
| Story 2.1.2 — `decide-cold-start-mitigation` → ADR-0001 | Cold start = 130s accepted; pivot criteria via 30-day review | Any i2i variant decision must re-evaluate cold-start budget |
| Story 2.2 — `typescript-sdk-flux-client` | SDK typed errors + retry logic | SDK needs new `generate({ image, prompt, ... })` overload or separate method |
| Story 2.5 — `gateway-rate-limit-cloudflare` | Worker gateway + KV + X-API-Key | Unchanged (passthrough) |
| ADR-0002 — `async-gateway-pattern` | POST /jobs + GET /jobs/{id}, opaque passthrough | i2i reuses this contract verbatim |

**No existing story covers i2i.** This analysis is the prerequisite to drafting one (via `@sm *draft`).

## 10. Summary — What's Ready to Reuse vs What Must Change

> **⚠️ Superseded by [ADR-0003](./adr-0003-image-to-image-model-selection.md) (2026-04-23, same day).** This section was written during the **v0.2 BFL-API exploration branch** and reflects the reuse/adapt surface *for that path*. The final decision (v0.3) is **self-hosted Qwen-Image-Edit on the existing RunPod endpoint** — it does **not** require `gateway/src/bfl.ts`, a `BFL_API_KEY` secret, or gateway multi-upstream routing. The actual reuse/adapt matrix for the accepted path is in [`recommended-approach.md`](./recommended-approach.md) §4.2–4.3. Table below retained as decision-history evidence, not as current guidance.
>
> **Prior-era history (for context only):** This table originally tracked the v0.1 Kontext-[dev] self-host plan (blocked by Non-Commercial license) → v0.2 BFL-API path (rejected for per-image vendor cost at portfolio scale).

| Component | Reuse as-is | Adapt | Create new |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cloudflare Worker gateway (`gateway/`) — auth + rate-limit | ✅ | — | — |
| `gateway/src/runpod.ts` | ✅ (T2I path) | — | — |
| Async contract (POST /jobs + GET /jobs/{id}) | ✅ (BFL uses same pattern) | — | — |
| `handler.py` (serverless) | ✅ (stays T2I only — untouched) | — | — |
| ComfyUI runtime + network volume + fp8 weights | ✅ | — | — |
| `gateway/src/index.ts` routing | — | **Yes** (branch on `input_image_b64` presence) | — |
| `gateway/src/types.ts:JobMapping` | — | **Yes** (add `upstream`, rename `runpod_request_id` → `upstream_request_id`, add optional `polling_url`) | — |
| `gateway/src/storage.ts` | — | **Yes** (serialize new fields; reader default for legacy records) | — |
| SDK `client.generate()` | ✅ (unchanged) | — | — |
| SDK new `edit()` method + `EditInput` type | — | — | **Yes** (v0.3.0 minor, additive) |
| Docs (`docs/api/reference.md`, SDK README) | — | **Yes** (add i2i variant; no breaking change to T2I) | — |
| `docs/legal/TERMS.md` + `PRIVACY.md` (+ .pt-BR) | — | **Yes** (BFL third-party processor disclosure) | — |
| ADRs | ✅ (ADR-0001, 0002 intact) | — | **Yes — ADR-0003** (BFL vendor decision + FLUX.2 [pro] selection) |
| Examples (`examples/colab/`) | — | — | Add i2i notebook after release |
| Tests (SDK + gateway) | ✅ framework | **Yes** (BFL mock tests for gateway; SDK tests for `edit()`) | — |
| **New: `gateway/src/bfl.ts`** | — | — | **Yes** — mirrors `runpod.ts` shape; submit + poll + error class |
| **New secret: `BFL_API_KEY`** | — | — | **Yes** — @devops via `wrangler secret put` |

## 11. External Integration Target — Black Forest Labs API

> **⚠️ Superseded by [ADR-0003](./adr-0003-image-to-image-model-selection.md) (2026-04-23, same day).** BFL is **not** the chosen i2i provider. This section is retained as **reference documentation** for two reasons: (a) transparent decision trail showing what was investigated and rejected, and (b) **potential future fallback** — the multi-provider gateway shape from `recommended-approach.md` §4.2 means a BFL upstream could be added later as a premium-tier or disaster-recovery route without re-architecture. Until then, **none of the code artifacts described below (bfl.ts, BFL_API_KEY, status mapping, signed-URL rehost) are being built**.
>
> Facts below still accurate as a vendor-integration reference if that fallback is ever activated.

This section captures the concrete surface that a future `gateway/src/bfl.ts` *could* integrate against. All facts sourced from [docs.bfl.ml](https://docs.bfl.ml), the [OpenAPI spec](https://api.bfl.ai/openapi.json), and the [pricing doc](https://docs.bfl.ml/quick_start/pricing.md) on 2026-04-23.

### 11.1 Endpoints

| Purpose | Method + path | Auth |
|---|---|---|
| Primary i2i model (BFL-recommended default for editing) | `POST https://api.bfl.ai/v1/flux-2-pro` | `x-key: ${BFL_API_KEY}` |
| Flat-price balanced variant | `POST https://api.bfl.ai/v1/flux-2-flex` | same |
| Premium tier | `POST https://api.bfl.ai/v1/flux-2-max` | same |
| Legacy Kontext — still supported | `POST https://api.bfl.ai/v1/flux-kontext-pro` · `POST /v1/flux-kontext-max` | same |
| Async result polling | `GET https://api.bfl.ai/v1/get_result?id={task_id}` | same |
| Account credits balance | `GET https://api.bfl.ai/v1/credits` → `{ credits: number }` | same |
| Finetune management (out of scope here) | `https://api.us1.bfl.ai` (regional) | same |

### 11.2 Submit request schema (Kontext-family and FLUX.2 are nearly identical)

```yaml
# Required
prompt: string

# Optional
input_image:   string          # base64 OR public URL — first reference image
input_image_2: string          # up to 4 reference images for Kontext; FLUX.2 [flex] up to 10
input_image_3: string
input_image_4: string
seed: integer
aspect_ratio: string           # range "21:9" to "9:21"
output_format: enum            # "jpeg" | "png" | "webp" (default "jpeg")
prompt_upsampling: boolean     # default false
safety_tolerance: integer      # 0-6 (default 2) — 0 strictest, 6 most permissive
webhook_url: string            # optional — alternative to polling
webhook_secret: string         # optional — HMAC for webhook auth
```

### 11.3 Submit response

```yaml
# AsyncResponse (when no webhook):
id: string
polling_url: string           # MUST be used for subsequent polls per BFL guidance
cost: number                  # credits consumed for this request
input_mp: number              # input megapixels processed
output_mp: number             # output megapixels produced

# AsyncWebhookResponse (when webhook_url provided):
id: string
webhook_url: string
# (no polling_url — async notifies via POST to our webhook_url)
```

### 11.4 Poll response (`GET /v1/get_result?id={id}`)

```yaml
id: string
status: enum                  # see 11.5
result: object | null         # populated when status=Ready
  # result.sample: string — signed URL to output image, 10-minute TTL
progress: number | null       # 0.0-1.0 (optional)
details: object | null
preview: object | null
```

### 11.5 Status values

| BFL `status` | Meaning | Terminal? |
|---|---|---|
| `Pending` | Job queued or running | No — keep polling |
| `Ready` | Complete — `result.sample` contains signed URL | Yes ✅ |
| `Error` | Generation failed (upstream) | Yes ❌ |
| `Request Moderated` | Prompt blocked by BFL moderation | Yes 🚫 |
| `Content Moderated` | Generated output blocked by BFL moderation | Yes 🚫 |
| `Task not found` | Invalid `id` or job evicted | Yes 🔍 (404-equivalent) |

### 11.6 Output delivery — ⚠️ signed URL with 10-minute TTL

`result.sample` is a signed URL BFL describes as *"not meant to be served directly to end users"*. Implication for our gateway: fetch-and-rehost on first `Ready` poll is mandatory if we want to preserve our existing AD-1 contract (image_b64 inline). See `recommended-approach.md` §5.1 for the three options evaluated.

### 11.7 Pricing (2026-04-23 snapshot)

All prices USD · 1 credit = $0.01.

| Model | Editing price / image | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| FLUX.2 [klein 4B] | from $0.014 | Free during preview ("Dev" tier shown as free) |
| FLUX.2 [klein 9B] | from $0.015 | — |
| **FLUX.2 [pro]** ⭐ | **from $0.045** | BFL recommended default for editing |
| FLUX.2 [flex] | $0.06 flat | — |
| FLUX.2 [max] | from $0.07 | Premium tier |
| FLUX.1 Kontext [pro] | $0.04 (4 credits) | Legacy; user's original reference |
| FLUX.1 Kontext [max] | $0.08 (8 credits) | Legacy premium |

**Megapixel scaling:** "The first megapixel costs a flat rate, then each additional MP adds to the total" — so 1024×1024 (~1 MP) is the cheapest tier; 2048×2048 (~4 MP) costs proportionally more on FLUX.2 models. Kontext pricing is flat.

### 11.8 Error handling per BFL guidance

- **429 Too Many Requests:** exponential backoff (`wait_time = 2 ** attempt`, up to 3 retries).
- **402 Payment Required:** insufficient credits — surface to gateway as `503 billing_preflight_failed` (no user-facing BFL leak).
- **4xx other:** treat as our `gateway_configuration_error` (500) until category is better-understood.
- **5xx:** map to `upstream_error` (502).

### 11.9 Data residency / moderation

- BFL runs inference with built-in NSFW + CSAM filtering (per their published content-safety posture).
- `safety_tolerance` is the only user-tunable moderation lever — default 2 on 0-6 scale.
- **Privacy implication:** prompts and input images transit BFL infrastructure. Disclosure required in TERMS/PRIVACY before shipping i2i (see `recommended-approach.md` §4.3).

### 11.10 Non-facts (explicitly unknown on 2026-04-23)

- BFL rate limits per API key — not documented in the pulled materials. Assume conservative behavior; observe in practice.
- BFL SLA / uptime commitments — not documented on public pages. Status dashboard exists but not linked in OpenAPI.
- Idempotency keys — not mentioned; assume none. Client-side dedup (if ever needed) is our responsibility.
- Data retention policy for submitted images/prompts — not on the integration guide; @pm to check before the TERMS disclosure language is finalized.

---
